name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: Income support scheme providing Rs 6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families across India, paid in three equal installments of Rs 2,000 each.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, former/present holders of constitutional posts, serving/retired government employees with pension above Rs 10,000, income tax payers, and professionals like doctors, engineers, lawyers, CAs.
benefits: Direct income support of Rs 6,000 per year transferred directly to bank account in 3 installments of Rs 2,000 each.
how_to_apply: Apply online at pmkisan.gov.in or visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC). State Nodal Officers also help with registration. Required: Aadhaar card, bank account details, and landholding records.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account (linked to Aadhaar), Land Ownership documents, Mobile number

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: Crop insurance scheme that provides financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops in notified areas. Both loanee and non-loanee farmers can apply.
benefits: Insurance coverage for crop loss due to natural calamities. Premium rates: 2% for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. Government pays remaining premium.
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest bank branch, CSC centre, or online at pmfby.gov.in before the cutoff date for each crop season.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account, Land records, Sowing certificate, Photo ID

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Provides farmers with affordable credit for agricultural needs including crop production, post-harvest expenses, and allied activities like dairy, fishery.
eligibility: All farmers, including individual/joint borrowers, tenant farmers, oral lessees, sharecroppers, self-help groups. Must be engaged in farming activities.
benefits: Credit limit based on land and crop. Interest rate of 4% per annum (with 3% subvention). Credit up to Rs 3 lakh at concessional rates. Also covers personal accident insurance.
how_to_apply: Apply at any commercial bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank branch. Online applications available through some bank portals.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Land ownership documents, Passport-size photos, Bank account

name: PM Scholarship Scheme
description: Scholarship for the wards and widows of ex-servicemen and ex-Coast Guard personnel for professional and technical education.
eligibility: Wards/widows of ex-servicemen of Armed Forces and Coast Guard. Student must be pursuing professional degree courses. Minimum 60% marks in qualifying exam.
benefits: Rs 3,000/month for boys, Rs 36,000/year. Rs 3,000/month for girls, Rs 36,000/year for select courses. Duration: 1-5 years depending on course.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the Kendriya Sainik Board (KSB) portal at ksb.gov.in during the application window (usually October-November).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ex-serviceman discharge certificate, Educational certificates, Bank Account, Passport-size photos

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship Scheme (NMMSS)
description: Scholarship to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to reduce dropout rates at the secondary and senior secondary level.
eligibility: Students of Class 9 to 12 studying in government or aided schools. Family income must be below Rs 3.5 lakh per annum. Must have scored minimum 55% in Class 8 exam.
benefits: Rs 12,000 per annum (Rs 1,000 per month) for students from Class 9 to 12.
how_to_apply: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in). State-level selection test is conducted.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income certificate, School marks sheet, Bank Account, Caste certificate (if applicable)

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Financial assistance for Scheduled Caste students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education to enable them to complete their education.
eligibility: Scheduled Caste students pursuing post-matric courses. Family income should not exceed Rs 2.5 lakh per annum. Must have passed previous qualifying exam.
benefits: Full tuition fee waiver for government institutions. Maintenance allowance from Rs 380 to Rs 1,200 per month based on course level and day-scholar/hostel status.
how_to_apply: Apply through the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in). Applications usually open in August-September.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste certificate, Income certificate, Previous year marks sheet, Bank Account, Institute bonafide certificate

name: Startup India Scheme
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Flagship initiative to build a strong ecosystem for nurturing innovation and startups in India, offering tax benefits, funding support, and regulatory simplification.
eligibility: Entity registered as Private Ltd, Partnership, or LLP. Age of entity up to 10 years from incorporation. Annual turnover not exceeding Rs 100 crore. Must be working towards innovation or scalable business model.
benefits: Tax exemption for 3 consecutive years (Section 80-IAC). Self-certification for compliance. Fast-track patent filing with 80% rebate. Fund of Funds (Rs 10,000 crore corpus). Easier public procurement norms.
how_to_apply: Register on startupindia.gov.in. Submit registration form with required documents for DPIIT recognition.
documents_required: Certificate of Incorporation, PAN, Brief description of innovation, Directors/Partners details, Website/App link

name: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY)
description: Provides loans up to Rs 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises for income-generating activities. Three categories: Shishu (up to Rs 50,000), Kishore (Rs 50,000 to Rs 5 lakh), Tarun (Rs 5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh).
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for a non-farm income-generating activity. No collateral required. Includes manufacturing, trading, services, and allied agriculture activities.
benefits: Collateral-free loans. Shishu: up to Rs 50,000. Kishore: up to Rs 5 lakh. Tarun: up to Rs 10 lakh. Competitive interest rates. No processing fee for Shishu loans.
how_to_apply: Apply at any commercial bank, RRB, cooperative bank, MFI, or NBFC-MFI branch. Online through mudra.org.in or Udyamimitra portal.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Business plan, Identity/address proof, Caste certificate (if applicable), Quotation for machinery/equipment

name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Campaign to address declining child sex ratio and promote the education and empowerment of the girl child through awareness and social mobilization.
eligibility: Families with girl children. The scheme operates at district level across India. Parents can open Sukanya Samriddhi Account for girls under 10 years.
benefits: Awareness campaigns and community mobilization. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (linked): savings account for girl child with attractive interest rate (currently around 8%). Tax benefits under Section 80C.
how_to_apply: Sukanya Samriddhi Account can be opened at any post office or participating bank. For awareness programs, contact District Magistrate or ICDS office.
documents_required: Birth certificate of girl child, Aadhaar Card of parent/guardian, Address proof, Passport-size photos

name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY)
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Maternity benefit programme providing Rs 5,000 in three installments to pregnant women and lactating mothers for the first living child.
eligibility: Pregnant women and lactating mothers for first living child. Not applicable to women receiving similar benefits under other schemes. Must register pregnancy at Anganwadi Centre or health facility.
benefits: Rs 5,000 in three installments: Rs 1,000 on early registration, Rs 2,000 after 6 months of pregnancy, Rs 2,000 after child birth registration and first cycle of vaccination.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or approved health facility. Forms available at AWC centres.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank/Post Office Account, MCP Card (Mother & Child Protection), Identity proof

name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: World's largest health insurance scheme providing coverage of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization across India.
eligibility: Families identified based on SECC (Socio-Economic Caste Census) 2011 data. No restrictions on family size, age, or gender. Both rural and urban deprived families covered.
benefits: Health coverage of Rs 5 lakh per family per year. Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals (both public and private). Covers 1,393+ treatment packages including surgeries, medical, and day care treatments. Pre and post hospitalization expenses covered.
how_to_apply: Check eligibility on mera.pmjay.gov.in or call 14555. Visit nearest Ayushman Bharat empanelled hospital or CSC to get e-card.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, SECC data verification, Any government ID

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
description: Accidental death and disability insurance scheme at a premium of just Rs 20 per year for all savings bank account holders aged 18-70 years.
eligibility: Savings bank account holders aged 18-70 years. Must have Aadhaar linked to bank account. Auto-debit consent for premium.
benefits: Rs 2 lakh for accidental death or permanent total disability. Rs 1 lakh for permanent partial disability. Annual premium of only Rs 20 auto-debited from bank account.
how_to_apply: Apply through your bank (SMS, net banking, or branch visit). Enrollment period: June 1 to May 31 each year.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Savings Bank Account, Nominee details

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: Life insurance scheme offering Rs 2 lakh life cover at a premium of Rs 436 per year for all savings bank account holders aged 18-50 years.
eligibility: Savings bank account holders aged 18-50 years. Must have Aadhaar linked to bank account. Auto-debit consent required.
benefits: Rs 2 lakh life insurance cover. Annual premium of Rs 436. Coverage for death due to any reason. Renewable annually.
how_to_apply: Apply through your bank via SMS, net banking, or branch visit. Premium auto-debited annually.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Savings Bank Account, Nominee details, Age proof

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: Government-backed pension scheme for unorganized sector workers, guaranteeing a minimum monthly pension of Rs 1,000 to Rs 5,000 after age 60.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-40 years with a savings bank account. Not an income tax payer. Not a member of any statutory social security scheme.
benefits: Guaranteed pension of Rs 1,000 to Rs 5,000 per month after age 60, depending on contribution. Government co-contributes 50% of premium (up to Rs 1,000/year) for 5 years for eligible accounts opened before Dec 2015. Spouse pension and return of corpus to nominee on death.
how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch or apply online through net banking. Aadhaar and mobile number required.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account, Mobile number

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
description: Provides financial assistance for construction of pucca houses to eligible rural houseless households and those living in kutcha/dilapidated houses.
eligibility: Houseless families or those living in kutcha/dilapidated houses in rural areas. Beneficiaries identified from SECC 2011 data verified by Gram Sabha. Priority: SC/ST, freed bonded labourers, minorities, single women.
benefits: Rs 1.20 lakh in plains and Rs 1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas. 90/95 person-days of unskilled labour under MGNREGA. Support for toilet construction from SBM-G.
how_to_apply: Beneficiaries are identified by Gram Panchayat from SECC data. Contact Gram Panchayat, Block Development Office, or District Rural Development Agency.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account, SECC Data verification, Land ownership proof, Photograph

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Affordable housing scheme for urban poor with interest subsidy on home loans. Targets housing for all in urban areas under four verticals.
eligibility: Economically Weaker Section (EWS, income up to Rs 3 lakh), Low Income Group (LIG, Rs 3-6 lakh), Middle Income Group I (MIG-I, Rs 6-12 lakh), MIG-II (Rs 12-18 lakh). Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house. Women ownership/co-ownership mandatory for EWS/LIG.
benefits: Interest subsidy: EWS/LIG - 6.5% on loan up to Rs 6 lakh. MIG-I - 4% on loan up to Rs 9 lakh. MIG-II - 3% on loan up to Rs 12 lakh. Subsidy upfront through CLSS.
how_to_apply: Apply online at pmaymis.gov.in or through CSC centres. Can also apply through banks and Housing Finance Companies.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income proof, Bank Account, Property documents, No pucca house certificate

name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to rural households whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
eligibility: Any adult member of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Must apply for a job card at the local Gram Panchayat.
benefits: 100 days guaranteed employment per household per year. Daily wage rate notified state-wise (Rs 200-350+). Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days. Worksite facilities: drinking water, shade, first aid.
how_to_apply: Apply for Job Card at Gram Panchayat. Submit written application for work to Gram Panchayat. Work to be provided within 15 days of application.
documents_required: Passport-size photograph, Age proof, Address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID), Bank Account

name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Skill certification scheme enabling Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training for better livelihood and employability.
eligibility: Indian nationals who are school/college dropouts or unemployed. Age: 15-45 years (varies by job role). No educational qualification required for some courses.
benefits: Free skill training in 300+ job roles. Certification by NSDC. Training allowance of Rs 150-200 per day in some courses. Placement assistance. Recognition of prior learning for experienced workers.
how_to_apply: Visit nearest PMKVY Training Centre or register at pmkvyofficial.org. Contact Skill India helpline: 1800-123-9626.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Educational certificates (if any), Bank Account, Passport-size photos

name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Promotes apprenticeship training in industry by sharing 25% of prescribed stipend (up to Rs 1,500/month) with employers for apprentice engagement.
eligibility: Youth aged 14+ years (varies by trade). Must have passed 5th/8th/10th/12th depending on trade category. Contract period: 6 months to 3 years.
benefits: Stipend support from government. On-the-job training. National trade certificate on completion. Industry exposure and potential employment.
how_to_apply: Register on apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. Search for opportunities and apply to employers directly through the portal.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Educational certificates, Bank Account, Photo ID

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Facilitates bank loans between Rs 10 lakh and Rs 1 crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up greenfield enterprises.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs. Age 18+ years. Setting up a greenfield (new) enterprise in manufacturing, services, or trading sector. Should not be defaulter to any bank.
benefits: Composite loan of Rs 10 lakh to Rs 1 crore (covers 75% of project cost). Repayment: up to 7 years. Moratorium period: up to 18 months. Margin money: 25% (can include convergence with other schemes).
how_to_apply: Apply online at standupmitra.in or visit any scheduled commercial bank branch.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Caste certificate (for SC/ST), Business plan, Identity/address proof, Quotations for machinery

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
description: Monthly pension for BPL persons aged 60 years and above under the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP).
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 60+ years belonging to BPL (Below Poverty Line) families. Must be verified by Gram Panchayat/Municipality.
benefits: Rs 200/month for age 60-79 years (Central share). Rs 500/month for age 80+ years. Most states add their own contribution making total Rs 400-2,000+ per month.
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat (rural) or Municipality (urban). Some states have online application through NSAP portal.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Age proof, BPL certificate/Ration card, Bank Account, Photograph

name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
description: Monthly pension for BPL widows aged 40-79 years under the National Social Assistance Programme.
eligibility: Widows aged 40-79 years from BPL families. Must not be receiving pension from any other scheme.
benefits: Rs 300/month (Central share). States add their own contribution. Total varies Rs 500-2,000 per month by state.
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat (rural) or Municipality (urban).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Death certificate of husband, Age proof, BPL certificate, Bank Account

name: Swachh Bharat Mission - Gramin (SBM-G)
description: Program to achieve universal sanitation coverage in rural areas by constructing individual household latrines and ensuring villages become Open Defecation Free (ODF).
eligibility: Rural households without a toilet. Priority: BPL families, SC/ST households, small/marginal farmers, physically handicapped, women-headed households.
benefits: Rs 12,000 incentive for construction of individual household latrine (IHHL). Additional support for construction of community sanitary complexes.
how_to_apply: Apply through Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office. Can also apply online through sbm.gov.in.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL card (if applicable), Bank Account, Photograph of house (without toilet)

name: Jal Jeevan Mission
description: Aims to provide functional household tap connection (FHTC) to every rural household by 2024, ensuring potable water supply of 55 litres per person per day.
eligibility: All rural households without piped water supply. Priority: SC/ST habitations, quality-affected areas, drought-prone areas, aspirational districts.
benefits: Functional household tap water connection with regular supply of prescribed quality in adequate quantity (55 lpcd).
how_to_apply: Implementation through village-level committees (VWSC/Pani Samiti). Contact Gram Panchayat or District Jal Jeevan Mission office.
documents_required: Household details through Gram Panchayat

name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Micro-credit scheme for street vendors to restart their livelihoods affected by COVID-19 with working capital loan up to Rs 50,000.
eligibility: Street vendors who have been vending on/before March 24, 2020. Must have a Certificate of Vending or letter of recommendation from ULB/TVC.
benefits: First loan: Rs 10,000. Second loan: Rs 20,000 (on repayment of first). Third loan: Rs 50,000. Interest subsidy of 7%. Monthly cashback incentive for digital transactions.
how_to_apply: Apply online at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in or through participating banks and ULBs.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Certificate of Vending/LoR from ULB, Bank Account, Passport-size photo

name: National Education Policy (NEP) - Samagra Shiksha
description: Integrated scheme for school education covering pre-school to Class 12, providing support for infrastructure, teaching quality, and inclusive education.
eligibility: Government and government-aided schools across India. Students in Classes 1-12 eligible for various sub-components (uniforms, textbooks, transport).
benefits: Free textbooks, uniforms for government school students. Teacher training and support. Digital infrastructure. Inclusive education for CWSN. Mid-day meals. Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya residential schools for girls.
how_to_apply: Benefits delivered through schools and state education departments. No individual application required for most components.
documents_required: School enrollment details

name: Ujjwala Yojana 2.0 (PMUY)
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from Below Poverty Line (BPL) households to ensure clean cooking fuel access and reduce health hazards from traditional fuels.
eligibility: Women aged 18+ from BPL households not having LPG connection in any family member's name. Adult woman of the household is the applicant.
benefits: Free LPG connection (security deposit for cylinder and regulator waived). First LPG refill provided free. Stove provided free in many states. EMI option for subsequent refills.
how_to_apply: Apply at nearest LPG distributor (Indane, HP Gas, Bharat Gas). Online application through ujjwalayojana.gov.in.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL ration card, Bank Account, Passport-size photo, Address proof

name: Saubhagya - Pradhan Mantri Sahaj Bijli Har Ghar Yojana
description: Provides free electricity connections to all willing un-electrified and willing households in rural areas and poor families in urban areas.
eligibility: All un-electrified households in rural areas. Poor households in urban areas identified through SECC 2011 data.
benefits: Free electricity connection including LED bulb, wiring, and meter for BPL households. APL households: Rs 500 connection charge (payable in 10 installments). Solar photovoltaic-based standalone system for remote areas.
how_to_apply: Apply through local electricity distribution company (DISCOM) or gram panchayat.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or any government ID, Address proof

name: Digital India Programme
ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
description: Umbrella programme to transform India into a digitally empowered society through digital infrastructure, digital services, and digital literacy.
eligibility: Various components have different eligibility. PMGDISHA (digital literacy): Rural households with no digitally literate family member. CSC (Common Service Centres): Entrepreneurs in villages.
benefits: Free digital literacy training (PMGDISHA). Government services delivered digitally (DigiLocker, UMANG app). BharatNet broadband connectivity to gram panchayats. CSC: digital services in every village.
how_to_apply: For PMGDISHA: Register at nearest digital literacy training centre. For DigiLocker: Download app or visit digilocker.gov.in.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Mobile number, Educational qualification details (for PMGDISHA)

name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
description: National financial inclusion mission ensuring access to banking services with at least one basic bank account for every household, along with financial literacy and insurance.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 10+ years who does not have a bank account. No minimum balance required.
benefits: Zero balance savings bank account. Free RuPay debit card. Accident insurance cover of Rs 2 lakh. Overdraft facility up to Rs 10,000 (after satisfactory operation for 6 months). Life insurance cover of Rs 30,000 for accounts opened during specified period.
how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch or Business Correspondent (Bank Mitra). Fill simplified KYC form.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or any government ID proof. If Aadhaar available, no other document required.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: Small savings scheme for the girl child with attractive interest rate and tax benefits, encouraging parents to save for their daughter's education and marriage.
eligibility: Parents/guardians of girl child below 10 years of age. Maximum 2 accounts per family (one per girl child). Minimum deposit: Rs 250/year.
benefits: High interest rate (currently around 8.2% p.a.). Tax benefits under Section 80C. Maturity after 21 years from account opening. Partial withdrawal (50%) allowed after girl turns 18 for education. Fully tax-free on maturity.
how_to_apply: Open account at any post office or participating bank branch.
documents_required: Birth certificate of girl child, ID proof of parent/guardian, Address proof, Passport-size photos

name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana
ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
description: End-to-end support for traditional artisans and craftspeople working with hands and tools in 18 identified trades through recognition, skill upgradation, and financial support.
eligibility: Artisans/craftsmen working in 18 trades: carpenter, blacksmith, goldsmith, potter, sculptor, cobbler, tailor, mason, boat maker, armorer, locksmith, hammer/toolkit maker, fishing net maker, doll/toy maker, barber, garland maker, washerman, broom maker. Age 18+ years. Should be engaged in the trade in unorganized sector.
benefits: PM Vishwakarma certificate and ID card. Skill training (5-7 days basic, 15 days advanced) with stipend of Rs 500/day. Collateral-free loans: Rs 1 lakh (first tranche) and Rs 2 lakh (second tranche) at 5% interest. Incentive for digital transactions. Marketing support.
how_to_apply: Register through CSC or pmvishwakarma.gov.in. Verification through Gram Panchayat/ULB.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account, Mobile number, Trade verification by Gram Panchayat/ULB

name: National Health Mission (NHM)
ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: Umbrella programme covering National Rural Health Mission and National Urban Health Mission to provide accessible, affordable healthcare to all, especially underserved communities.
eligibility: All Indian citizens, with focus on rural and urban poor. Various sub-schemes have specific eligibility (e.g., Janani Suraksha Yojana for pregnant women, RBSK for children 0-18 years).
benefits: Free treatment at government health facilities. Janani Suraksha Yojana: Cash incentive for institutional delivery. ASHA workers in every village. Mobile Medical Units. Free medicines and diagnostics at public facilities.
how_to_apply: Visit nearest government primary health centre, community health centre, or district hospital. No formal application needed for most services.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card or any ID proof for registration. BPL card for targeted schemes.

name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
target_group: Rural poor women, Self Help Groups
description: Aims to reduce poverty by enabling poor rural households to access gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment through Self Help Groups (SHGs).
eligibility: Rural BPL women. Must form or join a Self Help Group (SHG) of 10-20 members. Priority: vulnerable communities, SC/ST, minorities, disabled, landless.
benefits: Revolving fund: Rs 10,000-15,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund: up to Rs 2.5 lakh per SHG. Interest subvention on bank loans. Skill training and placement. Startup support for micro-enterprises.
how_to_apply: Contact Block/District Mission Management Unit or state rural livelihood mission. Join through local community mobilization.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, BPL identification, Bank Account (individual and SHG), Photo ID

name: Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS)
ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Provides a package of six services through Anganwadi Centres: supplementary nutrition, pre-school education, immunization, health check-up, referral services, and nutrition & health education.
eligibility: Children 0-6 years, pregnant women, and lactating mothers. Focus on those from disadvantaged backgrounds. Services delivered through Anganwadi Centres.
benefits: Free supplementary nutrition. Pre-school non-formal education for 3-6 year olds. Immunization. Health check-ups. Referral services. Nutrition and health education for women.
how_to_apply: Visit nearest Anganwadi Centre. Registration by Anganwadi Worker. No formal application required.
documents_required: Child's birth certificate, Mother's Aadhaar Card, Immunization card

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
description: Provides soil health cards to farmers every 2 years with crop-wise recommendations of nutrients and fertilizers to improve productivity and reduce input costs.
eligibility: All farmers with agricultural land. Cards issued free of charge. Soil samples collected by agricultural department.
benefits: Free soil health card with nutrient status of soil (N, P, K and micronutrients). Crop-wise fertilizer recommendations. Helps reduce fertilizer costs and improve yields.
how_to_apply: Register at nearest agriculture office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra. Online at soilhealth.dac.gov.in.
documents_required: Land details, Aadhaar Card, Mobile number

name: eShram - National Database of Unorganized Workers
description: National portal to register unorganized workers and create a comprehensive database to provide social security benefits and welfare schemes.
eligibility: Workers aged 16-59 years working in the unorganized sector. Should not be a member of EPFO or ESIC. Includes construction workers, gig workers, street vendors, domestic workers, agricultural workers.
benefits: eShram card as universal identity for unorganized workers. Accidental insurance: Rs 2 lakh (death/permanent disability), Rs 1 lakh (partial disability) under PMSBY. Future: seamless access to government welfare schemes.
how_to_apply: Self-registration at eshram.gov.in using Aadhaar and mobile number. Can also register through CSC centres.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card (linked to mobile number), Bank Account details, Work category details

name: Agnipath Scheme - Agniveer
description: Short-term recruitment scheme for Indian Armed Forces. Agniveers serve for 4 years with option for 25% to continue in permanent cadre.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 17.5-23 years. Physical and medical fitness as per armed forces standards. Educational qualification: 10th/12th pass depending on category.
benefits: Monthly salary starting Rs 30,000 (increasing annually). Seva Nidhi of Rs 11.71 lakh on completion of 4-year tenure (tax-free). Rs 48 lakh life insurance. Skill certificates. Preference in government jobs after tenure.
how_to_apply: Apply online at joinindianarmy.nic.in (Army), joinindiannavy.gov.in (Navy), or careerindianairforce.cdac.in (Air Force).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, 10th/12th mark sheets, Domicile certificate, Character certificate, Sports certificates (if any)

name: PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahaabhiyaan)
ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
description: Promotes solar energy among farmers through solar pumps and grid-connected solar power plants on barren/fallow land to ensure energy security and additional income.
eligibility: Individual farmers, farmer groups, cooperatives, panchayats, FPOs. Components: A) Solar plant on barren land, B) Standalone solar pumps, C) Solarisation of existing grid-connected pumps.
benefits: Component A: Income from selling solar power. Component B: Subsidy of 30% Central + 30% State for solar pumps up to 7.5 HP. Component C: Solarize existing pumps with similar subsidy. Farmer pays only 40% (can be through bank loan).
how_to_apply: Apply through state nodal agency or mnre.gov.in. Contact local agriculture/renewable energy office.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Land documents, Existing pump/electricity connection details (for Component C), Bank Account

name: National SC/ST Hub
description: Provides professional support to SC/ST entrepreneurs to grow their business through registration, public procurement, capacity building, and handholding.
eligibility: SC/ST entrepreneurs and aspiring entrepreneurs. MSMEs owned by SC/ST individuals.
benefits: Free registration on GeM and CPPP portals. Technical guidance for public procurement participation. Financial assistance for attending exhibitions. Vendor development programs. Capacity building workshops.
how_to_apply: Register at scsthub.in or visit nearest MSME Development Institute.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Caste certificate, Udyam Registration (for existing MSME), Business plan (for aspiring entrepreneurs)

name: Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY)
description: Health insurance scheme for BPL families in the unorganized sector providing coverage for hospitalization.
eligibility: BPL families, building and construction workers, MGNREGA workers, street vendors, domestic workers, and other unorganized sector worker categories.
benefits: Coverage of Rs 30,000 per family per year. Cashless hospitalization. Family floater basis (5 members). Smart card based. Pre-existing diseases covered from day one.
how_to_apply: Registration drives conducted by state government. Visit district labour office or CSC centre.
documents_required: BPL card/eShram card, Aadhaar Cards of family members, Ration card, Photo

name: Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)
ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution
description: Provides free foodgrains to all eligible households covered under the National Food Security Act (NFSA) to ensure food security.
eligibility: All beneficiaries covered under NFSA with Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) or Priority Household (PHH) ration cards.
benefits: 5 kg of foodgrains per person per month free of cost. AAY households: 35 kg per household per month. Includes rice, wheat, or coarse grains.
how_to_apply: Existing NFSA ration card holders automatically eligible. Collect foodgrains from nearest Fair Price Shop (FPS). New ration cards: apply at district food department or online through state PDS portal.
documents_required: Ration Card, Aadhaar Card (for biometric verification at FPS)

name: One Nation One Ration Card (ONORC)
ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution
description: Enables migrant workers and beneficiaries to access their entitled foodgrains from any Fair Price Shop across India through portability of ration cards.
eligibility: All NFSA beneficiaries (AAY and PHH ration card holders). Aadhaar must be linked to ration card.
benefits: Portability of ration card across India. Access foodgrains from any ePoS-enabled FPS nationwide. Particularly beneficial for migrant workers.
how_to_apply: Ensure Aadhaar is linked to ration card. Visit any ePoS-enabled FPS with ration card and Aadhaar for biometric authentication.
documents_required: Ration Card (linked to Aadhaar), Aadhaar Card

name: Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY)
description: Provides all-weather road connectivity to unconnected habitations in rural areas to improve rural infrastructure and accessibility.
eligibility: Unconnected eligible habitations in rural areas. Habitations with population of 500+ in plain areas and 250+ in hilly/tribal/desert areas.
benefits: All-weather roads connecting villages to market centres and social services. Roads built to specified standards ensuring durability.
how_to_apply: Road proposals submitted by state governments and district authorities. Citizens can raise demand through Gram Panchayat.
documents_required: Not applicable for individual citizens (infrastructure scheme)

name: Mid-Day Meal Scheme (PM POSHAN)
description: Provides free cooked meals to children in government and government-aided schools to improve nutritional levels and encourage school attendance.
eligibility: All students in Classes 1-8 in government and government-aided schools, EGS centres, NCLP schools, and Madrasas supported under SSA.
benefits: Free nutritious cooked lunch on every school day. Calorie norms: 450 calories and 12g protein for primary; 700 calories and 20g protein for upper primary. Cooking cost and foodgrains provided by Centre and State.
how_to_apply: Automatically available for enrolled students in eligible schools. No separate application needed.
documents_required: School enrollment

name: National Pension System (NPS)
description: Voluntary defined contribution pension system for all citizens to create a retirement corpus with market-linked returns and tax benefits.
eligibility: Indian citizens and NRIs aged 18-70 years. Two types: NPS Tier I (pension, restricted withdrawal) and NPS Tier II (savings, flexible withdrawal).
benefits: Tax deduction under Section 80CCD(1) up to Rs 1.5 lakh and additional Rs 50,000 under 80CCD(1B). Choice of fund managers and investment options. Partial withdrawal allowed for specific purposes. At least 40% of corpus used for annuity at retirement.
how_to_apply: Open account online at enps.nsdl.com or through Point of Presence (PoP) centres (banks, post offices).
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Bank Account, Passport-size photo, Address proof

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) 2.0
ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Extended version of Ujjwala covering migrant workers who can get connection with self-declaration, no requirement of address/ration card proof from native place.
eligibility: Adult woman from any poor household (BPL, most backward class). Migrant families can use self-declaration for address proof. No existing LPG connection in household.
benefits: Free LPG connection. First refill and stove provided free. Deposit-free connection. Self-declaration accepted for address proof.
how_to_apply: Apply at nearest LPG distributor. Migrant workers can apply with self-declaration of address at their current location.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Self-declaration of address (for migrants), Bank Account

name: UDID (Unique Disability Identification) Card
ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Universal ID card for persons with disabilities to ease access to government schemes and benefits through a single card across India.
eligibility: Any person with disability as defined under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016 (21 specified disabilities). Must be assessed by a Certified Medical Authority.
benefits: Universal ID recognized across India. Single card for accessing all disability-related schemes and benefits. Simplifies application processes. Digital record of disability percentage.
how_to_apply: Apply online at swavlambancard.gov.in. Visit nearest district hospital for disability assessment.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Medical certificate of disability, Passport-size photo, Address proof

name: Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY)
description: Pension scheme for senior citizens providing assured return on deposit with Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC).
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 60+ years. Maximum investment: Rs 15 lakh per senior citizen. Policy term: 10 years.
benefits: Assured pension of around 7.4% per annum (varies). Pension payable monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, or annually. Loan facility up to 75% of purchase price after 3 years. GST exemption on premium.
how_to_apply: Purchase online or offline from LIC. Visit nearest LIC branch or website licindia.in.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Age proof, Bank Account, Passport-size photo

name: National Rural Livelihood Mission - Aajeevika
description: Major rural poverty alleviation program organizing rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) for financial inclusion, skill development, and livelihood enhancement.
eligibility: Rural women from poor and vulnerable households. One woman per household can join an SHG. Priority: households with MGNREGA dependence, PwD, landless, migrant workers.
benefits: SHG formation and nurturing support. Revolving Fund of Rs 10,000-15,000 per SHG. Community Investment Fund up to Rs 2.5 lakh per SHG. Bank credit linkage facilitation. Skill training and micro-enterprise development.
how_to_apply: Contact Block Mission Management Unit (BMMU) or State Rural Livelihood Mission. Community mobilization by existing SHG members.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Bank Account (SHG account), Group resolution, Member list

name: Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for MSEs (CGTMSE)
description: Provides collateral-free credit facility to micro and small enterprises by guaranteeing loans up to Rs 5 crore through lending institutions.
eligibility: New and existing micro and small enterprises in manufacturing and service sectors. Individual proprietorship, partnership, LLC, trust, cooperative, private/public limited company. Should not be NPA/wilful defaulter.
benefits: Collateral-free loan up to Rs 5 crore. Guarantee cover: 75-85% of sanctioned credit. Covers term loan and/or working capital. Lower guarantee fee for women/NER entrepreneurs.
how_to_apply: Apply through any Member Lending Institution (MLI) - banks, NBFCs, SFCs, NEDFi. The bank applies for guarantee cover on behalf of borrower.
documents_required: Business plan, Udyam Registration, Financial statements, PAN/Aadhaar, Bank statements, Project report

name: Pradhan Mantri Rojgar Protsahan Yojana (PMRPY)
description: Incentivizes employers for new employment generation by paying employer's EPF/EPS contribution for new employees for 3 years.
eligibility: Establishments registered with EPFO. New employees earning up to Rs 15,000/month with new UAN and Aadhaar-linked bank account.
benefits: Government pays employer's full 12% EPF+EPS contribution for 3 years for each new employee. Reduces employer's cost of hiring. Employee gets social security cover.
how_to_apply: Employer registers on EPFO portal. Claims processed through EPFO online system.
documents_required: EPFO establishment registration, Employee UAN, Aadhaar details

name: Free Sewing Machine Scheme (State-Level)
description: Several state governments provide free sewing machines to women from economically weaker sections to promote self-employment and financial independence.
eligibility: Women aged 20-40 years from BPL families. Annual family income below Rs 12,000 in most states. Widows and disabled women get priority.
benefits: Free sewing machine for self-employment. Skill training in some states. Opportunity for home-based tailoring business.
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest government office or women welfare department. Some states have online portals.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card, Income certificate, Age proof, BPL certificate, Passport-size photos, Disability certificate (if applicable)
